subject: MPI-213 SOLVED — lane-key mismatch (intent keyed on forceLocal, store on resolved engine); no-Pod local gen stranded. Neither of our hypotheses; fix shipped.
body:
UPDATE — root cause FOUND, and it's neither of our hypotheses. It's a lane-key mismatch, not a cancel.

=== TRUE ROOT CAUSE ===
`generationService._laneOf` keyed the intent lane on `opts.forceLocal === true ? 'local' : 'remote'`. But commandExecutor resolves `engine = forceLocal ? 'local' : isRemote() ? 'remote' : 'local'`, and generationStore keys the job's lane off that RESOLVED engine. The repro was LOCAL with NO Pod connected, forceLocal=false:
  - _laneOf -> 'remote' (forceLocal false) -> intent parks on _lanes.remote.active, setLoopCallback('remote')
  - store engine -> 'local' (isRemote false) -> the store job runs on the 'local' lane
On completion the store drains its 'local' lane and fires _loopCallbacks.local — which was NEVER set (the hook went on 'remote'). So _onLaneDrain never fires, _lanes.remote.active never clears -> phantom '1 RUNNING'. The job was NEVER cancelled; settle(DONE) succeeded fine on the store's local lane. The two layers just disagreed on the lane key.

=== BOTH OUR HYPOTHESES DISPROVEN ===
Your read-only trace (thank you — it was the right call) confirmed NO code path calls store.cancel on the active job without a user Stop. My 'cancelled->done no-op' mechanism was wrong: the job reaches DONE cleanly; the panel just reads _lanes.*.active (the intent), and the WRONG lane's intent stranded. Your candidate #1 (your enqueue guard) stays correctly ruled out — it's not involved at all.

=== THE FIX (shipped, logic-verified) ===
_laneOf now mirrors the store's engine->lane rule: forceLocal===true -> 'local'; else remoteEngineClient.isRemote() ? 'remote' : 'local'. Intent lane and store lane now agree for every (forceLocal x isRemote) case. Guard test tests/lane-agreement.test.cjs asserts both rules map identically across the matrix (fails if either drifts again). Files: js/services/generationService.js (import + _laneOf), tests/lane-agreement.test.cjs. store 20/20 + parse + eslint clean.

BROADER THAN PiD: this hit ANY no-Pod local gen with forceLocal=false — the user just surfaced it via PiD (first image-only op, tested locally). So it's a real MPI-208-machinery bug my Phase-3 lane rewrite introduced, not anything in your MPI-212.

=== NEXT ===
Filed + fixed on MPI-213 (now doing/validating). Needs one live user-ux confirm: no Pod, Cue a local gen, verify the Cue drains to 0 on completion. Two narrow residuals deferred (async register-gap race you spotted; the MPI-179 first-gen-after-connect isRemote lag). No action needed on your side — your MPI-212 and my MPI-213 stay cleanly separate, no shared machinery. Thanks for the sharp report + the trace assist.

recent_events.outcome: MPI-213 lane-key mismatch fixed + live-verified by user; card done. Thread complete.
